steps.title: Find the antecedent
steps.content: The blank is the subject of "will begin to combust." The antecedent is "the substance," referring to coconut oil. That antecedent is singular.

steps.title: Use a singular subject pronoun
steps.content: Choice A and choice C are plural, so they do not agree with the singular antecedent. Choice D is a demonstrative that does not work as the natural pronoun for a previously named substance in this sentence. The remaining pronoun is singular and works as the subject: "it will begin to combust." The answer is B.
